channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: SpaceX gains 3% in premarket trading after 20% spike on Monday
item.description: CEO Elon Musk said Sunday that the company "might be able to reach approximately" $1 trillion revenue in 2030."

item.title: Brent oil dips below $80 per barrel for first time since March
item.description: Global oil prices tumbled to a three-month low on Tuesday as the Iran peace agreement dominated the G7 meeting in France.

item.title: Trump denies U.S. will put 'any money' into Iran, as he meets allies at G7 summit
item.description: Washington and Tehran announced a memorandum of understanding had been reached over the weekend.

item.title: Kevin Warsh's Fed is not expected to make any change to rates for a while, according to CNBC Fed Survey
item.description: The survey respondents do expect the Fed at this week's meeting to remove the easing bias in the statement that has signaled the Fed's next move would likely be a cut.

item.title: Here are Tuesday's biggest analyst calls: Apple, Tesla, Oracle, Palantir, CoreWeave, ExxonMobil, Flutter & more
item.description: Here are the biggest calls on Wall Street on Tuesday.

item.title: Property Play: How AI may be messing with home prices
item.description: For most real estate professionals, the data aggregation capabilities of AI can enhance their expertise. But there are shortcomings.

item.title: Trump turns his attention to Ukraine ahead of Iran deal: 'I’m going to do whatever I can’
item.description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday that "Russia should make a deal" to end its four-year war in Ukraine.

item.title: U.S. stock futures are flat after Dow record close on U.S.-Iran deal; SpaceX rallies again: Live updates
item.description: On Monday, the Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed to new intraday and closing records on a potential deal between the U.S. and Iran.

item.title: How Elon Musk's second-in-command Gwynne Shotwell helped turn SpaceX into an IPO giant
item.description: CNBC spoke to people who've worked with SpaceX's president and chief operating officer.

item.title: This cybersecurity stock could soar on AI tailwinds. UBS says to buy it now
item.description: Dynatrace could see considerable upside as it benefits from a combination of AI tailwinds, per UBS.

item.title: People in China are watching the World Cup differently this time
item.description: A 12-hour-plus time difference and various streaming options are influencing how people in China watch the World Cup.

item.title: How the Strait of Hormuz reopening could unfold if the U.S.-Iran deal is implemented
item.description: Tanker traffic through Hormuz could quickly increase but it is unclear whether transits will approach prewar levels, according to Kpler.

item.title: Yum Brands sells Pizza Hut to private equity firm LongRange Capital and Yum China for $2.7 billion
item.description: Yum Brands is selling Pizza Hut, capping off years of struggles for the pizza chain.

item.title: Trading platform Robinhood cuts 10% of workforce to flatten management layers
item.description: Robinhood said on Tuesday it would cut 10% ⁠of its full-time workforce, or about 290 roles, as the trading platform seeks to flatten ​management layers.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: SpaceX, Robinhood, Huntsman & more
item.description: These are the stocks posting the largest moves premarket.

item.title: Nvidia plans to raise at least $20 billion in its first debt sale since start of AI boom
item.description: Nvidia is set to raise capital in a debt sale for the first time since 2021, when the chipmaker was a fraction of its current size.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup could boost this struggling sportsbook owner, Wedbush says
item.description: FanDuel owner Flutter Entertainment is likely to see some upside from the FIFA World Cup and further expand its presence in the U.S., per Wedbush Securities.

item.title: Treasury yields fall ahead of Kevin Warsh's first Fed meeting
item.description: The yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note — the key benchmark for U.S. government borrowing — fell over 2 basis points to 4.449%.

item.title: CFTC chair Selig defends decision to approve ‘perps’ in U.S.
item.description: Michael Selig said that incumbents will always fear the future, but it's important to develop the new asset class domestically rather than only offshore.

item.title: Jim Cramer's top 10 things to watch in the stock market Tuesday
item.description: SpaceX is back at it again.

item.title: Qualcomm CEO says AI agents will replace apps — as chip giant works on 40 new AI-powered devices
item.description: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon said he is bullish on smart glasses which could eventually become as big as the smartphone.

item.title: This no-fee Amex checking account is offering a $300 bonus for a limited time — how to qualify
item.description: Open a new Amex Rewards Checking account by July 30 and receive at least $7,500 in direct deposits within 90 days to earn a $300 bonus.

item.title: Bank of Japan hikes rates to 1%, highest since 1995, as yen and inflation worries take hold
item.description: This is the BOJ's first hike since December, when it raised rates to its current level of 0.75%, also its highest level in over 30 years.

item.title: Gavin Newsom says Trump ordered DOJ to investigate him and his wife
item.description: California Gov. Newsom called President Donald Trump "simply the most corrupt President in American history."

item.title: Anthropic to meet with Trump administration over Mythos dispute
item.description: Anthropic received an export control directive that ordered the company to suspend access to its latest AI models by any foreign national.

item.title: To be America's Top State for Business in 2026, 'speed to market' wins
item.description: CNBC’s 2026 America’s Top States for Business rankings is a race as companies chase record investments in AI and defense in deciding where to locate.

item.title: Centene to offer buyouts to some employees as health insurer cuts costs
item.description: The health insurer Centene did not indicate to how many employees were offered buyouts or how much it is aiming to reduce its workforce.

item.title: Warsh's Fed is likely to hold rates steady — what the leadership change could mean for your money
item.description: The Federal Reserve's June meeting, the first helmed by new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh, may impact many consumer borrowing and savings rates down the road.

item.title: Dick's Sporting Goods expanding Lids shops to 100 locations
item.description: Dick's Sporting Goods announced a partnership to bring Lids shops to 100 Dick's stores.

item.title: China economy weakens further in May as retail sales post first drop in over three years
item.description: China's retail sales fell for the first time in more than three years in May, while urban investment contracted more than expected, adding to signs the economic slump deepened.
